errors.alreadyIssued: You've already given this person a wristband - each person should only get one.

checks.childWristband: The person is 8. They should get a child wristband, but got: {{got}}.
checks.teenWristband: The person is 15. They should get a teen wristband, but got: {{got}}.
checks.adultWristband: The person is 30. They should get an adult wristband, but got: {{got}}.
checks.seniorWristband: The person is 70. They should get a senior wristband, but got: {{got}}.
checks.boundary13: The person is 13. They should get a teen wristband (13 is not under 13), but got: {{got}}.
checks.boundary18: The person is 18. They should get an adult wristband (18 is not under 18), but got: {{got}}.
checks.boundary65: The person is 65. They should get a senior wristband (65 is not under 65), but got: {{got}}.

assignWristband.name: Assign the correct wristband
assignWristband.description: Get the person's age and give them the correct wristband: child (under 13), teen (13-17), adult (18-64), or senior (65+).

childAge8.name: Age 8 (child)
childAge8.description: An 8-year-old. They should get a child wristband.

teenAge15.name: Age 15 (teen)
teenAge15.description: A 15-year-old. They should get a teen wristband.

adultAge30.name: Age 30 (adult)
adultAge30.description: A 30-year-old. They should get an adult wristband.

seniorAge70.name: Age 70 (senior)
seniorAge70.description: A 70-year-old. They should get a senior wristband.

boundary13.name: Age 13 (teen)
boundary13.description: Exactly 13. They're a teen, not a child.

boundary18.name: Age 18 (adult)
boundary18.description: Exactly 18. They're an adult, not a teen.

boundary65.name: Age 65 (senior)
boundary65.description: Exactly 65. They're a senior, not an adult.

gettingAge.question: How do I get hold of the person's age?
gettingAge.answer: Call `getAge()` and put the result in a variable, e.g. `let age = getAge()`. Then you can compare `age` to numbers in your conditions.

structureForRanges.question: Which structure do I use when there are several age ranges?
structureForRanges.answer: An `if` / `else if` / `else` chain. Each branch handles one wristband, and only one branch will run.

branchOrder.question: Does the order of the branches matter?
branchOrder.answer: Yes. Start with the youngest (`age < 13`), then `age < 18`, then `age < 65`. Because each branch only runs if the earlier ones failed, you do not need to write things like `age >= 13 && age < 18`.

seniorsElse.question: What about the seniors at the end?
seniorsElse.answer: You do not need a condition for them. A plain `else` that calls `giveSeniorWristband()` catches everyone aged 65 and over.

getAge.description: Returns the **age** of the person waiting outside.
